CASE_A.description: Best multi-hop question where GraphRAG would outperform Naive RAG
CASE_A.expected_performance: GraphRAG should significantly outperform Naive RAG on multi-hop questions due to graph traversal capabilities

example_question.question: Ngành nào tại Đại học Khoa học Huế có chỉ tiêu cao nhất năm 2025?
example_question.ground_truth_answer: Ngành Công nghệ thông tin (Cử nhân) tại Đại học Khoa học Huế có chỉ tiêu cao nhất với 510 sinh viên năm 2025.
example_question.expected_graphrag_advantage: GraphRAG would traverse multiple program nodes to compare quotas and identify the highest one, while Naive RAG might miss the comparison across different programs.

CASE_A.analysis: EXPERIMENT_FAILED - Cannot run actual comparison due to Qdrant connectivity issues

CASE_B.description: Simple question where both systems should perform well
CASE_B.expected_performance: Both systems should perform well on simple fact-based questions

example_question.ground_truth_answer: Ngành Công nghệ thông tin tại Đại học Khoa học Huế có chỉ tiêu 510 sinh viên năm 2025.
example_question.expected_performance: Both systems should easily retrieve the exact quota information from the program description chunk.

CASE_C.description: Failure case where GraphRAG might perform worse than Naive RAG
CASE_C.expected_performance: No such case identified - GraphRAG should perform equal or better in all scenarios
CASE_C.analysis: Based on the test question design and architecture analysis, GraphRAG should not perform worse than Naive RAG on any question. However, potential failure scenarios could include: 1) Graph traversal overhead for simple queries, 2) Incorrect graph schema mapping, 3) Entity resolution errors. Since the experiment failed, we cannot validate these hypotheses.
